annotations.decision: 1505 gram product of 29-3/7 week gestational age born to a 28 year- old gravida I, para 0, II to I mother
The prenatal screen laboratories included blood type A positive, antibody negative, hepatitis B surface antigen negative, RPR nonreactive, rubella immune
GC negative but history of chlamydia positive but treated and since negative, HIV negative and GBS unknown
The maternal medical history was noticeable for significant obesity, hypertension and asthma
The pregnancy was also notable for gestational diabetes
insulin
The pregnancy was notable for premature rupture of membranes greater than 48 hours
On the day of the delivery mom presented with footling presentation and preterm labor
She received a single dose of betamethasone and Ampicillin
cesarean section
infant was delivered by cesarean section
emerged stunned and limp. She was brought to the warmer, dried and stimulated and suctioned.
She required positive pressure ventilation for 30 seconds before spontaneous respirations
The Apgar scores were 6 and 7 at one and five minutes respectively
The anterior fontanelle was open and flat
Palate and clavicle were intact
On auscultation there was fair aeration with coarse breathing sounds. Visible moderate retractions
Heart sounds were normal, regular rate and rhythm with no audible murmur and normal femoral pulses
The abdomen was soft, nontender with no masses
The genitalia had normal female genitalia appropriate for age.
Neurologically appeared appropriate with all extremities moving
The right leg appeared bruised and swollen
The anus was patent.

increased work of     breathing.

extubated
started on caffeine. Caffeine citrate was     discontinued on [**2106-3-22**].
Prior to the spell free period she had occasional spells     which included a few episodes of bradycardia with     feeding
She remained hemodynamically     stable.
On examination a soft intermittent murmur is     heard sometimes
She was initially kept     NPO and started on IV fluids and parenteral nutrition
feeds were started on day of life 2. She gradually     advanced on feeds and was up to full feed son day of life
She then gradually progressed on her calories up to 30     calories per ounce and then was gradually weaned down
taking Preemie Enfamil or breast milk 24     calories per ounce with minimum volume of 130 cc, all     p.o. feeds
She received phototherapy
Her blood type is A positive and Coombs     negative
She is receiving ferrous sulfate and     vitamin A
She     received Ampicillin and gentamicin for 7 days
Lumbar     puncture was performed on day of life 3. The results     showed protein of 175, glucose 53, 8 RBCs and 2 WBCs
Both the blood cultures and spinal fluid culture remained     no growth to date
7 day initial course of     antibiotics
sent urine for     CMV which remained no growth to date. Her urine was also     negative for CMV.
Baby Girl [**Known lastname 65753**] had head ultrasound done on     [**2-24**] on day of life 4 was within normal limits
A     follow up head ultrasound on [**2106-3-23**] showed     increased density of thalamic vessels which was     interpreted as mineralization of the blood vessels
sent urine for CMV which was     reported negative
A follow up head ultrasound on the day     of discharge, [**2106-4-7**] revealed similar findings of     increased mineralization of blood vessels
The patient on     examination appears appropriate with normal tone and     posture for age
Her neurological examination remains     nonfocal to date
We strongly recommend close follow up     with the infant follow up clinic to monitor her neuro     developmental outcome
because of these findings of     increased mineralization of the blood vessels
We also recommend a follow up MRI at 1     month of age.
Hearing screen was performed with automated auditory     brain stem responses on [**2106-4-6**] and she passed for     bilateral ears
She had 2 eye examinations, the first one     on [**2106-3-15**] revealed immature zone 2, ROP mature     zone 2 blood vessels
Follow up eye examination on [**4-2**] again revealed immature zone 3
she requires a     follow up appointment with ophthalmology in 2 weeks.
